question: When does a computed value recalculate?

options.text: Lazily: only when accessed, if dependencies have changed
Immediately after any observable change in the store
options.explanation: Computed recalculates lazily — only when someone accesses its value. If nobody reads the computed, there's no recalculation even if dependencies changed.

options.text: On a timer every 100ms
options.explanation: MobX doesn't use polling. Computed recalculates only on access when dependencies are marked as changed.

options.text: On every React component re-render
options.explanation: Computed is cached and independent of React re-renders. Recalculation happens only when dependent observables change.

question: What is comparer.structural used for in computed?

options.text: To compare results by content instead of reference, avoiding unnecessary re-renders
To speed up computed calculations
options.explanation: comparer.structural doesn't speed up calculations — it determines how new and old values are compared. If the content hasn't changed, subscribers won't be notified.

options.text: To convert computed into an observable
options.explanation: comparer doesn't change the value type. It determines whether the new value is considered different from the old one.

options.text: To deep-clone the computed result
options.explanation: comparer doesn't clone data. It compares the new result with the old one: if they're structurally equal, subscribers aren't notified.

question: Why shouldn't you perform side effects (localStorage, fetch) inside computed?

options.text: Computed must be a pure function — side effects are unpredictable due to lazy and cached recalculation
MobX will throw an error on side effects in computed
options.explanation: MobX doesn't check for side effects — the code will run, but the result will be unpredictable. The side effect executes only on computed access, not on every dependency change.

options.text: Computed doesn't have access to window and global APIs
options.explanation: Computed is a regular JavaScript function with access to all APIs. But side effects in it are an anti-pattern due to unpredictable execution timing.

options.text: Side effects in computed slow down React
options.explanation: The issue isn't speed but unpredictability: computed recalculates lazily and is cached, so the side effect might not execute when you expect.

question: What happens in a computed chain if only taxRate changes while items remain the same?

options.text: tax will recalculate (depends on taxRate)
total will recalculate (depends on tax)
subtotal will NOT recalculate (doesn't depend on taxRate)
All computed values recalculate because they're linked in a chain
options.explanation: MobX tracks dependencies precisely. subtotal depends only on items — if items haven't changed, subtotal doesn't recalculate, even if it's part of the chain.

question: What's the danger of using keepAlive: true in computed?

options.text: It can cause memory leaks if the store is long-lived and computed holds references to large data structures
Computed stops being cached
options.explanation: On the contrary: keepAlive: true enhances caching — the cache is preserved even without subscribers.

options.text: Computed recalculates on every access
options.explanation: keepAlive doesn't cancel caching. Computed still recalculates only when dependencies change.

options.text: It's impossible to remove the store from memory
options.explanation: The store can be removed, but keepAlive computed maintains subscriptions to dependencies even without observers, which can delay garbage collection for related data.
